review_comment:
I'd like to see a single place (presumably in` kv_cache.go`) where we figure out the definitive cache quantization and then just directly use that rather than make adjustments everywhere. That applies to both this file and `server.go`.

For example, what happens if flash attention is disabled (either by the user or another check)? Won't this underestimate the memory requirements?

Refactored this to be a bit clearer.

When flash attention is disabled, KVCacheQuantization() returns f16 as the cache type, which estimateKvCacheSize() calculates as 2 bytes per element (f16).
estimateKvCacheSize uses the environment variable as the input to its calculations, not the output of KVCacheQuantization - that's exactly my concern.
Updated this now to use the function to get the value.

@jessegross how is this? Iterated a bit with @sammcj on this one. There's a little bit of duplication we could eliminate but it gives some flexibility on logging only in `server.go` for example. I'm ok either way
There's a little bit of duplication but it's ok - I think we've beaten this to death.
To confirm: are there any model specific limitations to what cache type is supported? Or is that simplified now that f32 is not considered.
E.g. to embedding models support q4_0? Ok if so, just confirming

I believe technically you can load an embedding model with k/v cache quantisation but folks tend not to as it might impact the quality of the embeddings (hence why they're usually either f16 or q8_0).

This function originally had the check for embeddings and flash attention that are now the responsibility of the caller and is only called if SupportsFlashAttention is true - which is where the check for embeddings is done.

This triggers a warning if flash attention is enabled but KV cache type is left as the default because cacheType is the empty string. One option is to normalize the value that comes out of the environment variable so we don't have to check for default empty values everywhere.

There are several places where we check for both f16 and fp16 but here we will only accept f16. I think that's fine - there's no real reason to accept both but we might as well remove fp16 as an option from other places since we will never hit it.

I've gone through and:

- Removed references to fp16.
- Defaulted to f16, removing the need for the "" check.
- Added a warning if you're requested a quantised k/v cache but have FA disabled.

Thanks for the review @jessegross!
